data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-29058e2a">
    <title>ddx work: server path drops 5 CLI flags (opaque_passthrough, max-cost, request-timeout, min-power, max-power)</title>
    <description>
ddx work / ddx agent execute-loop has two execution paths: --local (inline) and default (submit to running server). The server path silently drops five CLI flags between client → server request → ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec → spawned worker subprocess. Most user-visible symptom: `ddx work --harness claude` fails when going through the server but works under --local, because the server validates the harness against its provider list instead of treating it as an opaque passthrough constraint.

USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM
- `ddx work --harness claude --local` works.
- `ddx work --harness claude` (server path) fails.
- Server path is supposed to be a transparent passthrough — same flags, same behavior.

ROOT CAUSE: chain of omissions

1. CLIENT — cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1608-1611 extracts maxCostUSD, requestTimeout, minPower, maxPower from the cobra command but cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627 (call to executeLoopWithServer) and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1941 (the function signature) do NOT include them. The four flags are dropped client-side before any HTTP call.

2. CLIENT → SERVER — opaque_passthrough IS sent in the worker spec at cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1948-1950, BUT:

3. SERVER REQUEST STRUCT — cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260 (handleStartExecuteLoopWorker) request struct is MISSING fields for OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower. Server JSON-decode silently drops the unknown fields.

4. SERVER → SPEC — cli/internal/server/server.go:2289-2303 (call to StartExecuteLoop) does not set OpaquePassthrough on the spec (since it never decoded it). MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower aren't even spec fields yet.

5. WORKER SPAWN — cli/internal/server/workers.go:656 hardcodes escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD instead of spec.MaxCostUSD. RequestTimeout override never applied.

6. VALIDATION DIVERGENCE — at cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1647-1652, --local correctly skips harness validation when treatPassthroughAsOpaque=true (the ddx work case). On the server path, the same skip never happens because OpaquePassthrough was lost in step 3 — so harness 'claude' goes through ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService which rejects it. **This is the user's observed bug.**

WHY THIS IS A REAL BUG (not "just configure the server")
The server should be a transparent passthrough — every CLI flag accepted by ddx work should produce identical behavior whether routed --local or via the server. Silently dropping flags violates the contract and produces confusing failures (the local path works, the server path doesn't, with no error explaining why).

FIX SHAPE
- Add fields to server request struct (server.go:2246-2260): OpaquePassthrough bool, MaxCostUSD float64, RequestTimeout string, MinPower int, MaxPower int
- Add parameters to executeLoopWithServer signature (agent_cmd.go:1941); pass maxCostUSD/requestTimeout/minPower/maxPower from caller (agent_cmd.go:1627)
- Add fields to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec where missing (workers.go:25-49) — MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower
- Populate spec from request in handler (server.go:2289-2303)
- Use spec values in runWorker (workers.go:656 cost cap; ~594-601 request-timeout override)

NOT IN SCOPE
- Reworking the client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is; just add fields).
- Changing the validation logic itself — only ensuring opaque_passthrough actually gates validation as intended.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: executeLoopWithServer signature extended to accept maxCostUSD, requestTimeout, minPower, maxPower; caller at line ~1627 passes them; opaque_passthrough already passed (verify present).
2. cli/internal/server/server.go: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker request struct decodes OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower; StartExecuteLoop call populates the spec with all five.
3. cli/internal/server/workers.go: ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec gains MaxCostUSD float64, RequestTimeout string, MinPower int, MaxPower int (OpaquePassthrough already exists). runWorker uses spec.MaxCostUSD (fallback to DefaultMaxCostUSD if zero) and applies spec.RequestTimeout / MinPower / MaxPower the same way the local path does.
4. The harness-validation skip at agent_cmd.go:1647-1652 is reachable on the server path: if OpaquePassthrough is true on the spec, the server-spawned worker subprocess receives --opaque-passthrough (or equivalent state) so its own preflight validation skips the harness check. End-to-end: `ddx work --harness claude` succeeds via the server path.
5. Table-driven test (workers_test.go or new file): for each of the 5 flags, assert the value submitted to the server appears unchanged in the persisted .ddx/workers/<id>/spec.json AND in the spawned worker subprocess argv. Use a fake spawner or argv-capture seam.
6. End-to-end test: spawn a worker via the server endpoint with --harness=claude, --max-cost=99, --request-timeout=42m, --min-power=2, --max-power=7. Assert all five values land in the worker spec on disk.
7. Manual verification: `ddx work --harness claude` (no --local) on a small queue completes at least one bead end-to-end without harness-rejection error.
8. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/server/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
9. Regression test: confirm --local path is unchanged (existing tests still pass).
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:server, kind:fix, observed-failure</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-e34994e2" base-rev="dd174d4ac0529ac46a42985281f3113fe54efa51" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260502T205432-6a763ee3"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260502T205432-6a763ee3/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260502T205432-6a763ee3/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260502T205432-6a763ee3/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260502T205432-6a763ee3/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260502T205432-6a763ee3/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output: You're out of extra usage · resets 6:20pm (America/New_York)
